input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6518 [Automation] javac compile fails for com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp:3.8.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: OkHttp's MockWebServer module is the official helper for testing HTTP/HTTPS/HTTP2 clients and may be useful if the agent elects to write full client/server interaction tests, but it is not required to fix the javac failure or to reach meaningful non-network/public API coverage.

Existing OkHttp metadata tests include Android/Jetty platform stubs and native-image charset build arguments; local Gradle/native-image verification remains authoritative if generated changes alter those areas.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OkHttp 3.8.1 resolves as the core HTTP client with only Okio as a required runtime dependency; Android and jsr305 are provided/optional, and the reported failure is an API-signature compile mismatch in an existing test rather than missing setup. Meaningful coverage is reachable with the normal scaffold and in-process tests without Docker, environment variables, or extra Maven dependencies.
